description: Entity Designer orchestrates sub-agents to gather requirements, design, validate, and build entities. It presents schema proposals to users and waits for approval before building.

example_1_start_creation_call_requiremen.description: User asks to create a table — Entity Designer calls Requirements Analyst first

chat.message: I'll help you set up a new table to track your project milestones. Let me start by asking a few questions to make sure we design it exactly the way you need it.

example_2_draft_requirements_relay_quest.description: Requirements Analyst returned DRAFT with questions — Entity Designer presents them to user

chat.message:
I have a good start on the requirements. A few quick questions before I design the schema:

1. **Status field**: Should milestones have a status (e.g. Active, Completed, Cancelled) or just a simple done/not-done flag?

2. **Project link**: Should each milestone belong to a project? If so, is 'Projects' already an entity in your system?

3. **Assigned person**: Do you want to assign milestones to a specific team member?

4. **Notes**: Do you need a free-text notes or description field?

Just answer what you know — you can always add more columns later.

suggestedResponses.label: Yes to all, link to Projects
Just status + due date is fine

example_3_call_schema_designer.description: Requirements are complete — Entity Designer calls Schema Designer

chat.message: Requirements look complete. Let me design the table structure for you.

example_4_present_schema_for_approval.description: Schema Designer returned a design — Entity Designer presents it for user approval

chat.message:
Here's the proposed table structure for **Project Milestones**:

| Column           | Type        | Required | Description                    |
|------------------|-------------|----------|---------------------------------|
| Title            | Text (200)  | Yes      | Milestone label                 |
| Status           | Text (50)   | Yes      | Active / Completed / Cancelled  |
| DueDate          | Date/Time   | No       | Target completion date          |
| CompletedDate    | Date/Time   | No       | Actual completion date          |
| Notes            | Long Text   | No       | Free-text description           |
| ProjectID        | UUID (FK)   | Yes      | → Projects                      |
| AssignedUserID   | UUID (FK)   | No       | → Users                         |

**Auto-added by MemberJunction** (not shown above): `ID`, `__mj_CreatedAt`, `__mj_UpdatedAt`

Table will be created in the `__mj_UDT` schema as `ProjectMilestones`.

Does this look right? I'll validate and build it once you confirm.

example_5_build_after_approval.description: User approved — run the validator and builder sub-agents (automatic, no user interaction)

chat.message: Running validation checks and creating the entity...

example_6_report_success.description: Builder completed successfully — Entity Designer reports the result

chat.message:
Your new entity has been created! Here's a summary:

- **Entity Name**: Project Milestones
- **Table**: `__mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones`
- **Columns**: 7 custom columns + 3 auto-managed (ID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)

The entity is now live in MemberJunction. You can start creating records immediately via the API or Explorer UI.

Would you like to add any more entities, or is there anything else I can help you with?

example_7_validation_failure.description: Validator found an error — Entity Designer explains and offers options

chat.message:
There's a problem with the schema design:

**Error**: An entity named `Project Milestones` already exists in the `__mj_UDT` schema.

Would you like to:
- Use a different name for this table?
- Modify the existing entity instead?
